102:   - Summary:
103:     The Swarm Dashboard is a Svelte 5 web UI served by `orch serve` (Go backend) that provides real-time monitoring of agent status, daemon health, and operational metrics.
104:     
105:     **Critical context (Option A+):** The dashboard is Dylan's (meta-orchestrator's) ONLY observability layer. He does not use CLI tools directly. Dashboard failure = Dylan is blind. This makes dashboard reliability tier-0 infrastructure. See orchestrator skill "Observability Architecture (Option A+)" section.
106:     
107:     The architecture uses a **two-mode design** (Operational/Historical) to separate daily coordination from deep analysis. SSE connections enable real-time updates but are constrained by HTTP/1.1's 6-connection limit. Progressive disclosure and stable sorting prevent information overload while maintaining scan-ability.
108:     
109:     ---
110:   - Critical Invariants:
111:     1. **Two-mode design is mutually exclusive** - Cannot show both Operational and Historical views simultaneously
112:     2. **SSE Events auto-connect, Agentlog is opt-in** - Connection pool management
113:     3. **beadsFetchThreshold controls remote queries** - 5+ ready issues triggers `bd ready` shell-out
114:     4. **Progressive disclosure via collapsed panels** - Event panels start collapsed, expand on click
115:     5. **Stable sort maintains scan-ability** - Agent order doesn't change unless status changes
116:     6. **Early filtering reduces payload size** - Backend filters before sending to frontend
117:     
118:     ---
119:   - Why This Fails:
120:     ### Failure Mode 1: Connection Pool Exhaustion
121:     
122:     **Symptom:** API fetches hang or timeout when SSE panels open
123:     
124:     **Root cause:** HTTP/1.1 allows only 6 connections per origin; SSE occupies slots
125:     
126:     **Why it happens:**
127:     - Events SSE (auto-connect): 1 slot
128:     - Agentlog SSE (auto-connect before fix): 1 slot
129:     - Remaining 4 slots for API fetches
130:     - If 5+ API requests concurrent, some block
131:     
132:     **Fix (Jan 5):** Made Agentlog SSE opt-in via Follow button, freeing 1 slot
133:     
134:     ### Failure Mode 2: Slow Dashboard Load with 100+ Agents
135:     
136:     **Symptom:** Dashboard takes 5-10 seconds to load with many agents
137:     
138:     **Root cause:** `/api/agents` endpoint performs expensive operations (OpenCode queries, beads parsing) synchronously
139:     
140:     **Why it happens:**
141:     - Each agent requires OpenCode session query
142:     - Full beads issue parsing for each agent
143:     - No caching, recomputed on every request
144:     
145:     **Fix (Jan 6):** Response caching with 2-second TTL, reduced load time to <1 second
146:     
147:     ### Failure Mode 3: Information Overload in Operational Mode
148:     
149:     **Symptom:** Users overwhelmed by full swarm map with 50+ agents
150:     
151:     **Root cause:** Single view tried to serve both daily coordination and deep analysis
152:     
153:     **Why it happens:**
154:     - Operational needs: "What's ready? What's broken?"
155:     - Historical needs: "Show me everything, all filters, full archive"
156:     - One view can't optimize for both
157:     
158:     **Fix (Jan 7):** Two-mode design - Operational (focused) vs Historical (comprehensive)
159:     
160:     ### Failure Mode 4: Plugin Cascade (Dashboard "Disconnected" Despite Services Running)
161:     
162:     **Symptom:** Dashboard shows "disconnected", `overmind status` shows all 3 services running, but `orch status` returns HTTP 500
163:     
164:     **Root cause:** OpenCode plugin error (e.g., v1→v2 API incompatibility) crashes OpenCode's internal request handling
165:     
166:     **Why it happens:**
167:     - OpenCode loads plugins at startup
168:     - Bad plugin throws error on every request
169:     - `/api/agents` calls OpenCode → gets 500
170:     - Dashboard can't fetch agent data → shows "disconnected"
171:     - overmind sees process running (not crashed) → reports "running"
172:     
173:     **Cascade:**
174:     ```
175:     Plugin error → OpenCode internal 500 → orch status fails → API can't get agents → Dashboard "disconnected"
176:     ```
177:     
178:     **Fix (Jan 14):** Disable plugins, restart OpenCode, re-enable one-by-one. Root cause was session-resume.js using v1 API (object export) instead of v2 (function export).
179:     
180:     **Key insight:** Dashboard can appear "down" while all processes are technically "running". Health checks must verify data flow, not just port availability.
181:

209:   - See: /Users/dylanconlin/Documents/personal/orch-go/.kb/models/dashboard-agent-status.md
210:   - **STALENESS WARNING:**
211:     This model was last updated 2026-01-12.
212:     Deleted files: pkg/status/calculate.go, pkg/dashboard/server.go.
213:     Verify model claims about these files against current code.
214:   - Summary:
215:     The dashboard calculates agent status through a **Priority Cascade model**: check Phase first (highest priority), then registry state, then session existence. Status can be "wrong" at the dashboard level while being "correct" at each individual check - this is a measurement artifact, not a system failure. The root issue was observation infrastructure (missing events, double-counting metrics, state not surfaced), not broken completion logic.
216:     
217:     ---
218:   - Critical Invariants:
219:     1. **Phase: Complete is agent's declaration** - Only agent can reach this, not orchestrator
220:     2. **Registry is source of truth for abandonment** - Human judgment, can't be inferred
221:     3. **Session may outlive completion** - Session existence ≠ agent still working
222:     4. **Status checks don't mutate state** - Calculation is read-only, no side effects
223:     
224:     ---
225:   - Why This Fails:
226:     ### Failure Mode 1: "Dead" Agents That Completed
227:     
228:     **Symptom:** Dashboard shows "dead", but work is done and beads issue closed
229:     
230:     **Root cause:** Session cleanup happens async, dashboard checks session existence as fallback
231:     
232:     **Why it happens:**
233:     - Agent reaches Phase: Complete
234:     - `orch complete` verifies and closes beads issue
235:     - Session cleanup happens later (or not at all)
236:     - Dashboard cascade reaches session check → sees no session → "dead"
237:     
238:     **Fix (Jan 8):** Priority Cascade puts Phase check before session check
239:     
240:     ### Failure Mode 2: Metrics Show Wrong Completion Rate
241:     
242:     **Symptom:** `orch stats` showed 72% completion when reality was 89%
243:     
244:     **Root cause:** Metrics counted events (double-counting) instead of deduplicating entities
245:     
246:     **Why it happens:**
247:     - `agent.completed` event emitted by both `orch complete` AND beads close hook
248:     - Metrics counted events → some completions counted 2x
249:     - Result: inflated total, deflated completion %
250:     
251:     **Fix (Jan 8):** Metrics deduplicate by beads_id before calculating ratios
252:     
253:     ### Failure Mode 3: Work Completed via Bypass Paths
254:     
255:     **Symptom:** Beads issue closed but no completion event, dashboard doesn't update
256:     
257:     **Root cause:** `bd close` (direct) doesn't emit events, only `orch complete` does
258:     
259:     **Why it happens:**
260:     - Multiple paths to completion: `orch complete`, `bd close`, `bd sync` with commit message
261:     - Only `orch complete` emits events
262:     - Other paths are invisible to observation infrastructure
263:     
264:     **Fix (Jan 6):** Beads close hook emits `agent.completed` event
265:

308:   - Summary:
309:     Agent state exists across **four independent layers** (tmux windows, OpenCode in-memory, OpenCode on-disk, beads comments). These layers fall into two distinct categories: **state layers** (beads, workspace files) that represent what work was done, and **infrastructure layers** (OpenCode sessions, tmux windows) that represent transient execution resources. The dashboard reconciles these via a **Priority Cascade**: check beads issue status first (highest authority), then Phase comments, then registry state, then session existence. Status can appear "wrong" at the dashboard level while being "correct" at each individual layer - this is a measurement artifact from combining multiple sources of truth.
310:     
311:     ---
312:   - Critical Invariants:
313:     1. **Phase: Complete is agent's declaration** - Only agent can reach this, not orchestrator
314:     2. **Beads issue closed = canonical completion** - All status queries defer to beads
315:     3. **Session existence ≠ agent still working** - Sessions persist indefinitely
316:     4. **Status checks don't mutate state** - Calculation is read-only, no side effects
317:     5. **Multiple sources must be reconciled** - No single source has complete truth
318:     6. **Tmux windows are UI layer only** - Not authoritative for state
319:     
320:     ---
321:   - Why This Fails:
322:     ### Failure Mode 1: Dashboard Shows "Active" When Agent is Done
323:     
324:     **Symptom:** Dashboard shows agent as active, but `bd show <id>` says status=closed
325:     
326:     **Root cause:** Dashboard caching or SSE lag - hasn't received beads update yet
327:     
328:     **Why it happens:**
329:     - Agent reaches Phase: Complete
330:     - `orch complete` closes beads issue
331:     - Beads issue status = closed
332:     - Dashboard hasn't refreshed or polled beads yet
333:     - Dashboard still shows cached "active" state
334:     
335:     **Fix:** Refresh dashboard browser tab (forces beads query)
336:     
337:     **NOT the fix:** Deleting OpenCode session (treats symptom, not cause)
338:     
339:     ### Failure Mode 2: "Dead" Agents That Actually Completed
340:     
341:     **Symptom:** Dashboard shows "dead", but work is done and beads issue closed
342:     
343:     **Root cause:** Session cleanup happened before dashboard queried, cascade reached session check
344:     
345:     **Why it happens:**
346:     - Agent completed, beads issue closed
347:     - Session cleanup ran (manual or automatic)
348:     - Dashboard cascade: beads check → no issue (closed) → session check → no session → "dead"
349:     
350:     **Fix (Jan 8):** Priority Cascade puts beads/Phase check before session existence check
351:     
352:     ### Failure Mode 3: Agent Went Idle But Not Complete
353:     
354:     **Symptom:** Session status is "idle" but no `Phase: Complete` comment
355:     
356:     **Root cause:** Agent ran out of context, crashed, or didn't follow completion protocol
357:     
358:     **Why it happens:**
359:     - Session exhausts context (150k tokens)
360:     - Agent stops responding
361:     - SSE event: `session.status = idle`
362:     - No `Phase: Complete` was ever written
363:     - Dashboard shows "idle" or "waiting"
364:     
365:     **This is expected behavior.** Session idle ≠ work complete. Only agents that explicitly run `bd comment <id> "Phase: Complete"` are considered done.
366:     
367:     **Fix:** Check workspace for what agent accomplished, then either:
368:     - `orch complete <id> --force` if work is done
369:     - `orch abandon <id>` if work is incomplete
370:     
371:     ### Failure Mode 4: Cross-Project Agents Not Visible
372:     
373:     **Symptom:** Agent spawned with `--workdir /other/project` doesn't appear in dashboard
374:     
375:     **Root cause:** Dashboard only scans current project's `.orch/workspace/` directory
376:     
377:     **Why it happens:**
378:     - Workspace created in `/other/project/.orch/workspace/`
379:     - Dashboard running from `orch-go` only sees `orch-go/.orch/workspace/`
380:     - Cross-project discovery requires querying OpenCode sessions for unique directories
381:     
382:     **Fix (Jan 6):** Multi-project workspace cache built from OpenCode session directories

531:     if (pinnedIds.has(depNode.node.id)) return
532: 
533:     let prefix = ''
534:     if (depNode.depDepth > 0) {
535:       for (let i = 0; i < ancestorIsLast.length - 1; i++) {
536:         prefix += ancestorIsLast[i] ? '    ' : '│   '
537:       }
538:       const isLast = ancestorIsLast[ancestorIsLast.length - 1]
539:       prefix += isLast ? '└── ' : '├── '
540:     }
541: 
542:     items.push({ node: depNode.node, prefix, depDepth: depNode.depDepth })
543: 
544:     const visibleChildren = depNode.depChildren.filter(
545:       (c) => !pinnedIds.has(c.node.id),

547:     for (let i = 0; i < visibleChildren.length; i++) {
548:       const childIsLast = i === visibleChildren.length - 1
549:       walk(visibleChildren[i], [...ancestorIsLast, childIsLast])
550:     }
551:   }
552: 
553:   for (const root of chain.roots) {

507: 					const treeIdx = flattenedNodes.findIndex((n) => 
508: 						!isWIPItem(n) && (n as TreeNode).id === beadsId
509: 					);
510: 					if (treeIdx !== -1) {
511: 						selectedIndex = treeIdx;
512: 						scrollToSelected();
513: 					}
514: 				}
515: 				break;
516: 
517: 			case 'w':
518: 				event.preventDefault();
519: 				// Jump from tree item (if in WIP) to its WIP section position
520: 				if (!isWIP) {
521: 					const nodeId = (current as TreeNode).id;
522: 					if (pinnedTreeIds.has(nodeId)) {
523: 						// Find matching WIP item in flattenedNodes
524: 						const wipIdx = flattenedNodes.findIndex(n => {
525: 							if (!isWIPItem(n)) return false;
526: 							const wipItem = n as WIPItem;
527: 							if (wipItem.type === 'running') {
528: 								return (wipItem.agent.beads_id || wipItem.agent.id) === nodeId;
529: 							} else {
530: 								return wipItem.issue.id === nodeId;
531: 							}
532: 						});
533: 						if (wipIdx !== -1) {
534: 							selectedIndex = wipIdx;
535: 							scrollToSelected();
536: 						}
537: 					}
538: 				}
539: 				break;
540: 			
541: 			case 'c':
542: 				event.preventDefault();
543: 				// Copy selected item's ID to clipboard
544: 				const id = getItemId(current);
545: 				copyToClipboard(id);
546: 				break;
547: 			
548: 			case 'p':
549: 				event.preventDefault();
550: 				// Enter priority mode (only for TreeNode, not WIP)
551: 				if (!isWIP) {
552: 					enterPriorityMode();
553: 				}
554: 				break;
555: 			
556: 			case '0':
557: 			case '1':
558: 			case '2':
559: 			case '3':
560: 			case '4':
561: 				event.preventDefault();
562: 				// Set priority (only when in priority mode and on TreeNode)
563: 				if (priorityMode && !isWIP) {
564: 					const priority = parseInt(event.key, 10);
565: 					await setPriority(current as TreeNode, priority);
566: 					exitPriorityMode();
567: 				}
568: 				break;
569: 			
570: 			case 'q':
571: 				event.preventDefault();
572: 				// Toggle triage:ready label (only for TreeNode, not WIP)
573: 				if (!isWIP) {
574: 					await toggleTriageReady(current as TreeNode);
575: 				}
576: 				break;
577: 		}
578: 	}

580: 	// Scroll selected item into view
581: 	function scrollToSelected() {
582: 		const element = document.querySelector(`[data-node-index="${selectedIndex}"]`);
583: 		if (element) {
584: 			element.scrollIntoView({ behavior: 'smooth', block: 'nearest' });
585: 		}
586: 	}

588: 	// Focus management - auto-focus on mount
589: 	let containerElement: HTMLDivElement;
590: 
591: 	onMount(() => {
592: 		// Use setTimeout to ensure DOM is fully rendered
593: 		setTimeout(() => {
594: 			containerElement?.focus();
595: 		}, 100);
596: 	});

598: 	// Toggle expansion
599: 	function toggleExpansion(node: TreeNode) {
600: 		node.expanded = !node.expanded;
601: 		// Notify parent component to update expansion state
602: 		onToggleExpansion(node.id, node.expanded);
603: 		// Manually rebuild flattened nodes (include WIP items + groups)
604: 		const items: (TreeNode | WIPItem | GroupHeader | DepSectionHeader)[] = [...wipItems];
605: 		if (isGrouped && groups.length > 0) {
606: 			for (const group of groups) {
607: 				items.push({
608: 					_groupHeader: true,
609: 					key: group.key,
610: 					label: group.label,
611: 					count: group.nodes.length,
612: 					unlabeled: group.unlabeled,
613: 				});
614: 				if (!collapsedGroups.has(group.key)) {
615: 					items.push(...flattenVisibleTree(group.nodes, pinnedTreeIds));
616: 				}
617: 			}
618: 		} else if (depPrefixMap.size > 0) {
619: 			// In dep chain mode, rebuild with chain structure
620: 			const dv = buildDependencyView(treeNodeIndex, edges);
621: 			const prefixes = new Map<string, string>();
622: 			for (const chain of dv.chains) {
623: 				items.push({ _depSectionHeader: true, key: chain.id, label: chain.label, count: chain.size, type: 'chain' as const });
624: 				if (!collapsedChains.has(chain.id)) {
625: 					const flatItems = flattenDepChain(chain, pinnedTreeIds);
626: 					for (const fi of flatItems) { prefixes.set(fi.node.id, fi.prefix); items.push(fi.node); }
627: 				}
628: 			}
629: 			if (dv.independentNodes.length > 0) {
630: 				items.push({ _depSectionHeader: true, key: 'independent', label: 'Independent Issues', count: dv.independentNodes.length, type: 'independent' as const });
631: 				if (!collapsedChains.has('independent')) {
632: 					for (const n of dv.independentNodes) { if (!pinnedTreeIds.has(n.id)) items.push(n); }
633: 				}
634: 			}
635: 			depPrefixMap = prefixes;
636: 		} else {
637: 			items.push(...flattenVisibleTree(tree, pinnedTreeIds));
638: 		}
639: 		flattenedNodes = items;
640: 		// Clamp selected index to valid range
641: 		if (selectedIndex >= flattenedNodes.length) {
642: 			selectedIndex = Math.max(0, flattenedNodes.length - 1);
643: 		}
644: 	}

646: 	// Select node on click
647: 	function selectNode(index: number) {
648: 		selectedIndex = index;
649: 	}

651: 	// Close side panel
652: 	function closeSidePanel() {
653: 		selectedIssueForPanel = null;
654: 	}
655: 	
656: 	// Open side panel for a node
657: 	function openSidePanel(node: TreeNode) {
658: 		selectedIssueForPanel = node;
659: 	}

661: 	// Handle close modal cancel
662: 	function handleCloseModalCancel() {
663: 		issueToClose = null;
664: 		// Restore focus to tree container for keyboard navigation
665: 		setTimeout(() => containerElement?.focus(), 0);
666: 	}
667: 
668: 	// Handle close modal confirm
669: 	async function handleCloseModalConfirm(event: CustomEvent<{ reason: string }>) {
670: 		if (!issueToClose || isClosing) return;
671: 
672: 		isClosing = true;
673: 		const projectDir = $orchestratorContext?.project_dir;
674: 		const result = await closeIssue(issueToClose.id, event.detail.reason, projectDir);
675: 
676: 		if (!result.success) {
677: 			console.error('Failed to close issue:', result.error);
678: 			// TODO: Show error toast
679: 		}
680: 
681: 		issueToClose = null;
682: 		isClosing = false;
683: 		// Restore focus to tree container for keyboard navigation
684: 		setTimeout(() => containerElement?.focus(), 0);
685: 	}

339: 
340:   const ageText = formatIssueAge(node.created_at)
341:   return {
342:     text: ageText ? `No active agent linked · ${ageText}` : 'No active agent linked',
343:     tone: 'text-amber-500/90',
344:   }

347: export function getPriorityVariant(
348:   priority: number,
349: ): 'destructive' | 'secondary' | 'outline' {
350:   if (priority === 0) return 'destructive'
351:   if (priority === 1) return 'secondary'
352:   return 'outline'
353: }

355: export function getTypeBadge(type: string): string {
356:   switch (type.toLowerCase()) {
357:     case 'epic':
358:       return 'bg-purple-500/10 text-purple-500'
359:     case 'feature':
360:       return 'bg-blue-500/10 text-blue-500'
361:     case 'bug':
362:       return 'bg-red-500/10 text-red-500'
363:     case 'task':
364:       return 'bg-green-500/10 text-green-500'
365:     case 'question':
366:       return 'bg-yellow-500/10 text-yellow-500'
367:     default:
368:       return 'bg-muted text-muted-foreground'
369:   }
370: }

372: export function getAgentStatusIcon(agent: any): { icon: string; color: string } {
373:   const health = computeAgentHealth(agent)
374: 
375:   if (health.status === 'critical') {
376:     return { icon: '🚨', color: 'text-red-500' }
377:   }
378:   if (health.status === 'warning') {
379:     return { icon: '⚠️', color: 'text-yellow-500' }
380:   }
381: 
382:   if (agent.is_processing) {
383:     return { icon: '◉', color: 'text-blue-500 animate-pulse' }
384:   }
385:   if (agent.status === 'idle') {
386:     return { icon: '⏸', color: 'text-muted-foreground' }
387:   }
388:   return { icon: '▶', color: 'text-blue-500' }

489: 			// Only react to actual project changes (not other context changes)
490: 			if (newProjectDir !== currentProjectDir) {
491: 				// Cancel any pending debounced fetch
492: 				if (projectChangeDebounceTimeout) {
493: 					clearTimeout(projectChangeDebounceTimeout);
494: 				}
495: 				
496: 				// Cancel any in-flight workGraph requests immediately

499: 				// Update state synchronously to prevent stale comparisons

505: 				// Load seen issues for this project from localStorage

509: 					// New project we haven't seen before - will be populated on first fetch
510: 					previousIssueIds = new Set<string>();
511: 				}
512: 				
513: 				// Debounce the actual fetch to wait for stable project value
514: 				// 300ms prevents rapid flip-flopping while still feeling responsive
515: 				projectChangeDebounceTimeout = setTimeout(() => {
516: 					projectChangeDebounceTimeout = null;
517: 					workGraph.fetch(newProjectDir, 'open', focusedBeadsId).catch(console.error);
518: 					attention.fetch(newProjectDir).catch(console.error); // Re-fetch attention for new project
519: 					// Also re-fetch kbArtifacts if we're in artifacts view
520: 					if (currentView === 'artifacts' && $kbArtifacts) {
521: 						kbArtifacts.fetch(newProjectDir).catch(console.error);
522: 					}
523: 					if (currentView === 'artifacts' && $kbModelProbes) {
524: 						kbModelProbes.fetch(newProjectDir).catch(console.error);
525: 					}
526: 				}, 300);
527: 			}
528: 		}

part.text:
**Refining ready-to-complete filtering**

Identified the need to ensure ready-to-complete only includes issues with status open/in_progress and phase complete, filtering out closed and stale ones by adding guards based on issue node status and source in the page logic.

**Planning UI refinements and sorting logic**

Planning to add stronger visual emphasis for in-progress issues, sort independent issues by priority and recency with a collapsed preview plus "Show all," clean up unassigned agent sublines, and refine ready-to-complete filtering to exclude stale entries by checking agent activity and issue status. Also clarifying dependency chain headers to use the topmost root issue title based on tree depth for accurate labeling.

204: 			update((agents) =>
205: 				agents.map((a) => (a.id === id ? { ...a, ...changes } : a))
206: 			);
207: 		},
208: 		removeAgent: (id: string) => {
209: 			update((agents) => agents.filter((a) => a.id !== id));
210: 		},
211: 		// Fetch agents from orch-go API with in-flight tracking
212: 		// Only one fetch runs at a time. If called while fetching, queues a re-fetch
213: 		// after the current one completes (prevents request storm from SSE events).
214: 		// Optional queryString parameter for time/project filtering (e.g., "?since=12h&project=orch-go")
215: 		async fetch(queryString: string = ''): Promise<void> {
216: 			// If already fetching, mark that we need another fetch after this one
217: 			if (isFetching) {
218: 				needsRefetch = true;
219: 				return;
220: 			}
221: 			
222: 			isFetching = true;
223: 			needsRefetch = false;
224: 			currentFetchController = new AbortController();
225: 			
226: 			try {
227: 				const response = await fetch(`${API_BASE}/api/agents${queryString}`, {
228: 					signal: currentFetchController.signal
229: 				});
230: 				// Update cache validation from response headers
231: 				cacheValidation.updateFromResponse(response);
232: 				if (!response.ok) {
233: 					throw new Error(`HTTP ${response.status}: ${response.statusText}`);
234: 				}
235: 				const data = await response.json();
236: 				// Transform API response: current_activity comes as string from backend,
237: 				// but frontend expects object {type, text, timestamp}
238: 				const transformed = (data || []).map((agent: Agent & { current_activity?: string | Agent['current_activity'], last_activity_at?: string }) => {
239: 					if (typeof agent.current_activity === 'string' && agent.current_activity) {
240: 						return {
241: 							...agent,
242: 							current_activity: {
243: 								type: 'text' as const,
244: 								text: agent.current_activity,
245: 								timestamp: agent.last_activity_at ? new Date(agent.last_activity_at).getTime() : Date.now()
246: 							}
247: 						};
248: 					}
249: 					return agent;
250: 				});
251: 				set(transformed);
252: 			} catch (error) {
253: 				// Don't log abort errors - they're expected during cleanup
254: 				if (error instanceof Error && error.name === 'AbortError') {
255: 					return;
256: 				}
257: 				console.error('Failed to fetch agents:', error);
258: 				throw error;
259: 			} finally {
260: 				currentFetchController = null;
261: 				isFetching = false;
262: 				
263: 				// If events arrived while we were fetching, do another fetch
264: 				// This ensures we don't miss updates without creating request storms
265: 				if (needsRefetch) {
266: 					needsRefetch = false;
267: 					this.fetchDebounced();
268: 				}
269: 			}
270: 		},
271: 		// Debounced fetch - prevents multiple rapid fetches from SSE events
272: 		fetchDebounced(): void {
273: 			if (fetchDebounceTimer) {
274: 				clearTimeout(fetchDebounceTimer);
275: 			}
276: 			fetchDebounceTimer = setTimeout(() => {
277: 				fetchDebounceTimer = null;
278: 				// Use filter query string if available
279: 				const queryString = getFilterQueryString ? getFilterQueryString() : '';
280: 				this.fetch(queryString).catch(console.error);
281: 			}, FETCH_DEBOUNCE_MS);
282: 		},
283: 		// Cancel pending operations - call on disconnect
284: 		cancelPending(): void {
285: 			if (currentFetchController) {
286: 				currentFetchController.abort();
287: 				currentFetchController = null;
288: 			}
289: 			if (fetchDebounceTimer) {
290: 				clearTimeout(fetchDebounceTimer);
291: 				fetchDebounceTimer = null;
292: 			}
293: 			// Reset in-flight tracking state
294: 			isFetching = false;
295: 			needsRefetch = false;
296: 		}
297: 	};
298: }

381: // SSE event stream with deduplication
382: // Events with the same part.id are updated in place rather than added as duplicates
383: function createSSEStore() {
384: 	const { subscribe, update } = writable<SSEEvent[]>([]);
385: 
386: 	return {
387: 		subscribe,
388: 		// Add or update an event - data is the raw parsed SSE event from OpenCode
389: 		addOrUpdateEvent: (data: any) => {
390: 			// Extract stable ID from part.id if available (for message.part events)
391: 			const partId = extractEventId(data);
392: 			
393: 			update((events) => {
394: 				const eventWithId: SSEEvent = {
395: 					id: partId || generateSSEEventId(),
396: 					type: data.type || 'unknown',
397: 					properties: data.properties,
398: 					timestamp: Date.now()
399: 				};
400: 				
401: 				// If we have a stable part.id, try to update existing event
402: 				if (partId) {
403: 					const existingIndex = events.findIndex(e => e.id === partId);
404: 					if (existingIndex !== -1) {
405: 						// Update in place - this replaces the old version with new data
406: 						const newEvents = [...events];
407: 						newEvents[existingIndex] = eventWithId;
408: 						return newEvents;
409: 					}
410: 				}
411: 				
412: 				// New event - add to list
413: 				const newEvents = [...events, eventWithId];
414: 				// Keep last 1000 events (global across all agents)
415: 				return newEvents.slice(-1000);
416: 			});
417: 		},
418: 		// Legacy addEvent for backwards compatibility
419: 		addEvent: (event: Omit<SSEEvent, 'id'>) => {
420: 			update((events) => {
421: 				const newEvents = [...events, { ...event, id: generateSSEEventId() }];
422: 				// Keep last 1000 events (global across all agents)
423: 				return newEvents.slice(-1000);
424: 			});
425: 		},
426: 		clear: () => {
427: 			update(() => []);
428: 		}
429: 	};
430: }

434: // Per-session event history store for hybrid SSE + API architecture.
435: // Stores historical events fetched from API separately from real-time SSE events.
436: // This enables:
437: // 1. Complete session history on-demand (survives page refresh)
438: // 2. Per-agent history without global buffer dilution
439: // 3. Seamless merge with real-time SSE events
440: interface SessionHistory {
441: 	events: SSEEvent[];
442: 	loading: boolean;
443: 	loaded: boolean;
444: 	error: string | null;
445: }
446: 
447: function createSessionHistoryStore() {
448: 	const { subscribe, update } = writable<Map<string, SessionHistory>>(new Map());
449: 	
450: 	return {
451: 		subscribe,
452: 		// Fetch historical events for a session from the orch-go API
453: 		async fetchHistory(sessionId: string): Promise<SSEEvent[]> {
454: 			// Check if already loaded or loading
455: 			let currentState: Map<string, SessionHistory> = new Map();
456: 			update(state => {
457: 				currentState = state;
458: 				return state;
459: 			});
460: 			
461: 			const existing = currentState.get(sessionId);
462: 			if (existing?.loaded || existing?.loading) {
463: 				return existing.events;
464: 			}
465: 			
466: 			// Mark as loading
467: 			update(state => {
468: 				const newState = new Map(state);
469: 				newState.set(sessionId, { events: [], loading: true, loaded: false, error: null });
470: 				return newState;
471: 			});
472: 			
473: 			try {
474: 				const response = await fetch(`${API_BASE}/api/session/${sessionId}/messages`);
475: 				if (!response.ok) {
476: 					throw new Error(`HTTP ${response.status}: ${response.statusText}`);
477: 				}
478: 				const events: SSEEvent[] = await response.json();
479: 				
480: 				// Store the fetched events
481: 				update(state => {
482: 					const newState = new Map(state);
483: 					newState.set(sessionId, { events, loading: false, loaded: true, error: null });
484: 					return newState;
485: 				});
486: 				
487: 				return events;
488: 			} catch (error) {
489: 				const errorMsg = error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'Unknown error';
490: 				update(state => {
491: 					const newState = new Map(state);
492: 					newState.set(sessionId, { events: [], loading: false, loaded: false, error: errorMsg });
493: 					return newState;
494: 				});
495: 				console.error('Failed to fetch session history:', error);
496: 				return [];
497: 			}
498: 		},
499: 		// Get current state for a session
500: 		getState(sessionId: string): SessionHistory | undefined {
501: 			let state: SessionHistory | undefined;
502: 			update(current => {
503: 				state = current.get(sessionId);
504: 				return current;
505: 			});
506: 			return state;
507: 		},
508: 		// Clear history for a session (useful when session is deleted)
509: 		clearSession(sessionId: string): void {
510: 			update(state => {
511: 				const newState = new Map(state);
512: 				newState.delete(sessionId);
513: 				return newState;
514: 			});
515: 		},
516: 		// Clear all history
517: 		clear(): void {
518: 			update(() => new Map());
519: 		}
520: 	};
521: }

537: // SSE connection manager - uses shared service for connection lifecycle
538: let sseConnection: SSEConnection | null = null;

540: // Build event listeners for the SSE connection
541: function buildSSEEventListeners(): Record<string, (event: MessageEvent) => void> {
542: 	const listeners: Record<string, (event: MessageEvent) => void> = {};
543: 
544: 	// Handle specific event types if sent with event: prefix
545: 	const eventTypes = ['session.status', 'session.created', 'session.deleted', 'agent.completed', 'agent.abandoned'];
546: 	eventTypes.forEach((type) => {
547: 		listeners[type] = (event: MessageEvent) => {
548: 			try {
549: 				const data = JSON.parse(event.data);
550: 				handleSSEEvent({ ...data, type });
551: 			} catch (e) {
552: 				console.error(`Failed to parse ${type} event:`, e);
553: 			}
554: 		};
555: 	});
556: 
557: 	// Handle custom events from our proxy
558: 	listeners['connected'] = (event: MessageEvent) => {
559: 		try {
560: 			const data = JSON.parse(event.data);
561: 			sseEvents.addEvent({
562: 				type: 'proxy.connected',
563: 				timestamp: Date.now()
564: 			});
565: 			console.log('SSE proxy connected to:', data.source);
566: 		} catch (e) {
567: 			console.log('SSE connected');
568: 		}
569: 	};
570: 
571: 	listeners['disconnected'] = () => {
572: 		connectionStatus.set('disconnected');
573: 		sseEvents.addEvent({
574: 			type: 'proxy.disconnected',
575: 			timestamp: Date.now()
576: 		});
577: 	};
578: 
579: 	listeners['error'] = (event: MessageEvent) => {
580: 		try {
581: 			const data = JSON.parse(event.data);
582: 			console.error('SSE proxy error:', data.error);
583: 			sseEvents.addEvent({
584: 				type: 'proxy.error',
585: 				timestamp: Date.now()
586: 			});
587: 		} catch (e) {
588: 			// Ignore parse errors for error events
589: 		}
590: 	};
591: 
592: 	return listeners;
593: }

603: export function connectSSE(): void {
604: 	// Create connection if not exists
605: 	if (!sseConnection) {
606: 		sseConnection = createSSEConnection(`${API_BASE}/api/events`, {
607: 			onOpen: () => {
608: 				connectionStatus.set('connected');
609: 				// Fetch agents on connection to get current state (with filters if available)
610: 				const queryString = getFilterQueryString ? getFilterQueryString() : '';
611: 				agents.fetch(queryString).catch(console.error);
612: 			},
613: 			onMessage: (event: MessageEvent) => {
614: 				try {
615: 					const data = JSON.parse(event.data);
616: 					handleSSEEvent(data);
617: 				} catch (e) {
618: 					// Non-JSON data, create simple event
619: 					sseEvents.addEvent({
620: 						type: 'raw',
621: 						timestamp: Date.now()
622: 					});
623: 				}
624: 			},
625: 			onDisconnect: () => {
626: 				connectionStatus.set('disconnected');
627: 			},
628: 			eventListeners: buildSSEEventListeners(),
629: 			reconnectDelayMs: 5000,
630: 			autoReconnect: true
631: 		});
632: 
633: 		// Sync the connection status from the service to our local store
634: 		sseConnection.status.subscribe((status) => {
635: 			connectionStatus.set(status);
636: 		});
637: 	}
638: 
639: 	// Mark as connecting and initiate connection
640: 	connectionStatus.set('connecting');
641: 	sseConnection.connect();
642: }

644: function handleSSEEvent(data: any) {
645: 	// Use addOrUpdateEvent for proper deduplication of streaming events
646: 	// Events with the same part.id will update in place rather than duplicate
647: 	sseEvents.addOrUpdateEvent(data);
648: 
649: 	// Handle message.part and message.part.updated events - update agent activity in real-time
650: 	// message.part: text streaming (incremental text chunks)
651: 	// message.part.updated: tool state changes (running -> completed)
652: 	if ((data.type === 'message.part' || data.type === 'message.part.updated') && data.properties) {
653: 		const part = data.properties.part;
654: 		// sessionID is nested inside the part object
655: 		const sessionID = part?.sessionID;
656: 		
657: 		if (sessionID && part && part.type) {
658: 			// Update agent activity and set is_processing=true (agent is actively responding)
659: 			// Only update active agents to prevent stale pulsing on completed agents
660: 			// (late SSE events can arrive after agent status changes to completed)
661: 			agents.update((agentList) => {
662: 				return agentList.map((agent) => {
663: 					// Match by session_id AND only update active agents
664: 					if (agent.session_id === sessionID && agent.status === 'active') {
665: 						return {
666: 							...agent,
667: 							is_processing: true, // Agent is actively generating response
668: 							current_activity: {
669: 								type: part.type,
670: 								text: part.text || extractActivityText(part),
671: 								timestamp: Date.now()
672: 							}
673: 						};
674: 					}
675: 					return agent;
676: 				});
677: 			});
678: 		}
679: 	}
680: 
681: 	// Handle session.status events - update is_processing based on busy/idle state
682: 	// Uses debounced clear to prevent rapid flapping between busy/idle states
683: 	if (data.type === 'session.status' && data.properties) {
684: 		const sessionID = data.properties.sessionID;
685: 		const statusType = data.properties.status?.type;
686: 		
687: 		if (sessionID && statusType) {
688: 			const isProcessing = statusType === 'busy';
689: 			
690: 			if (isProcessing) {
691: 				// Setting to busy: immediate, and cancel any pending clear timer
692: 				const pendingClear = processingClearTimers.get(sessionID);
693: 				if (pendingClear) {
694: 					clearTimeout(pendingClear);
695: 					processingClearTimers.delete(sessionID);
696: 				}
697: 				
698: 				agents.update((agentList) => {
699: 					return agentList.map((agent) => {
700: 						if (agent.session_id === sessionID && agent.status === 'active') {
701: 							return { ...agent, is_processing: true };
702: 						}
703: 						return agent;
704: 					});
705: 				});
706: 			} else {
707: 				// Setting to idle: debounced to prevent rapid flapping
708: 				// Cancel any existing timer first
709: 				const existingTimer = processingClearTimers.get(sessionID);
710: 				if (existingTimer) {
711: 					clearTimeout(existingTimer);
712: 				}
713: 				
714: 				// Set new timer for delayed clear
715: 				const timer = setTimeout(() => {
716: 					processingClearTimers.delete(sessionID);
717: 					agents.update((agentList) => {
718: 						return agentList.map((agent) => {
719: 							if (agent.session_id === sessionID) {
720: 								return {
721: 									...agent,
722: 									is_processing: false
723: 									// Note: Don't clear current_activity here - the last activity
724: 									// should persist so users can see what the agent was doing.
725: 									// Clearing it causes "Starting up..." to show for completed agents.
726: 								};
727: 							}
728: 							return agent;
729: 						});
730: 					});
731: 				}, PROCESSING_CLEAR_DELAY_MS);
732: 				
733: 				processingClearTimers.set(sessionID, timer);
734: 			}
735: 		}
736: 	}
737: 
738: 	// Handle lifecycle events - refresh agent list (debounced to prevent race conditions)
739: 	// Note: session.status is NOT included because it's already handled via local state
740: 	// updates above (lines 510-562). Including it would cause redundant fetches since
741: 	// session.status fires on every busy/idle toggle (high frequency).
742: 	const refreshEvents = [
743: 		'session.created',
744: 		'session.deleted',
745: 		'agent.completed',
746: 		'agent.abandoned'
747: 	];
748: 	if (refreshEvents.includes(data.type)) {
749: 		agents.fetchDebounced();
750: 	}
751: }

753: // Extract displayable text from message part
754: function extractActivityText(part: any): string {
755: 	// For tool invocations, show tool name and function
756: 	if (part.type === 'tool-invocation' || part.type === 'tool') {
757: 		if (part.tool) {
758: 			return `Using ${part.tool}${part.function ? `.${part.function}` : ''}`;
759: 		}
760: 		return 'Using tool';
761: 	}
762: 	
763: 	// For step-start/finish, show step info
764: 	if (part.type === 'step-start') {
765: 		return 'Starting step...';
766: 	}
767: 	if (part.type === 'step-finish') {
768: 		return 'Completed step';
769: 	}
770: 	
771: 	// For reasoning, show preview
772: 	if (part.type === 'reasoning' && part.text) {
773: 		return part.text.substring(0, 100);
774: 	}
775: 	
776: 	// Default: show part type
777: 	return part.type.replace(/-/g, ' ');
778: }

780: // Create a new beads issue (for follow-ups from synthesis recommendations)
781: export async function createIssue(title: string, description?: string, labels?: string[]): Promise<{ id: string; title: string } | null> {
782: 	try {
783: 		const response = await fetch(`${API_BASE}/api/issues`, {
784: 			method: 'POST',
785: 			headers: {
786: 				'Content-Type': 'application/json',
787: 			},
788: 			body: JSON.stringify({
789: 				title,
790: 				description,
791: 				labels: labels || ['triage:ready'],
792: 				issue_type: 'task',
793: 			}),
794: 		});
795: 		
796: 		if (!response.ok) {
